FsDryCheckStore.docs: Filesystem adapter implementing DryCheckReader and DryCheckWriter for dry-check.json. The on-disk format is a schema-versioned envelope object { schema_version: u32, records: Vec<DryCheckRecord> } — a private serde DTO (DryCheckJsonV1) internal to the infrastructure module, mirroring ReviewJsonV2 { schema_version, scopes } from FsReviewStore. Uses atomic write and fs4 file locking (mirrors FsReviewStore). DryCheckReader.read_records() parses the envelope and returns the records Vec (returns empty Vec if file absent, mirroring FsReviewStore.read_doc returning ReviewJsonV2::empty() on NotFound). DryCheckWriter.append_record(entry: &DryCheckEntry) acquires an exclusive lock, reads the envelope under lock (returning an empty envelope if the file is absent — init-on-first-write, matching FsReviewStore.append_round calling read_doc_for_write which returns ReviewJsonV2::empty() on NotFound), stamps Timestamp::new(infrastructure::timestamp_now()), constructs DryCheckRecord from DryCheckEntry + Timestamp, appends it to the records Vec, and writes the updated envelope back atomically. The interactor passes DryCheckEntry (7 fields, no recorded_at) — the adapter is the sole owner of recorded_at stamping, ensuring recorded_at is always a valid non-empty ISO-8601 UTC Timestamp (Finding 3). No separate init() method is needed: the first append creates the file. History is append-only at the records level; latest-per-pair wins by array position. Sole writer of dry-check.json — enforces D11 schema-constrained verdict ingestion.

CodexDryChecker.docs: Infrastructure adapter implementing DryCheckAgentPort via Codex CLI with --output-schema (structured verdict output). Invokes the dry-checker capability for a (changed-fragment, candidate-fragment) pair. Analogous to CodexReviewer.

DryCheckCommitHashError.docs: Error from FsDryCheckCommitHashStore.read(). CN-01: independent of domain::review_v2::CommitHashError (review gate interface). Three failure modes: I/O error reading the file, symlink detected below trusted_root, or invalid commit hash format. An absent file returns Ok(None) so NotFound is not an error variant.

GitDryCheckDiffGetter.docs: Infrastructure adapter implementing DryCheckDiffSource (dry-check's own diff-source port, method list_changed_hunks). CN-01: this is NOT GitDiffGetter from review_v2 — it is a dry-check-owned adapter. Returns Vec<DiffFileHunks>, each carrying a FilePath and a non-empty Vec<DiffHunkRange> of added/changed hunk line ranges (D4 hunk-scope). Behavior mirrors GitDiffGetter (merge-base + 4-source union via SystemGitRepo: committed diff merge-base..HEAD, staged, unstaged, untracked), but uses git diff --unified=0 (or equivalent) to extract hunk-level line ranges. SystemGitRepo is neutral git plumbing (not a gate interface) and is shared. Uses domain::review_v2::FilePath (shared domain value object, not a gate interface).

FsDryCheckCommitHashStore.docs: Infrastructure adapter for reading the per-track .commit_hash file to resolve the diff base for dry-check. CN-01: this is NOT FsCommitHashStore from review_v2 — it is a dry-check-owned adapter. Behavior mirrors FsCommitHashStore.read(): reads the file, validates it as a CommitHash via CommitHash::try_new(), then uses SystemGitRepo (merge-base --is-ancestor) to check ancestry. Three outcomes: (1) file absent → Ok(None) (main-tip fallback); (2) stored content is an invalid hash format → Err(DryCheckCommitHashError::Format) (propagated from CommitHash::try_new()); (3) hash is not an ancestor of HEAD → Ok(None) (fail-closed, main-tip fallback). Used at composition level by the CLI to resolve base_commit before calling DryCheckService.run_dry_check(). Not wired through a usecase port — the CLI calls it directly and passes the resolved CommitHash as a parameter.
